# Azure Service Bus Management Connector - Full Documentation

# Azure Service Bus Management 1.0 Additional Configuration Information

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Azure Service Bus Management (Azure Service Bus Management Connector) cannot
perform a connectivity test at startup using the **Test Connection** button in the global element of
the connector configuration when you restrict access to your resources and you have a security
policy with permissions at the resource level only. This scenario occurs because the security policy
targets the root level of the namespace, which might be forbidden due to the customized policy
applied to the shared access key.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Azure Service Bus Management 1.0 Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The examples in this section show how to get information about Azure Service Bus queues and how to
create an Azure Service Bus topic. These examples use variables for some field values. You can
either:

- Replace the variables with their values in the code
- Provide the values for each variable in a properties file and then refer to that file from the
  connector configuration

  If you donât know how to use a properties file, see
  [Configuring Property Placeholders](../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-app-properties-to-configure)

## Get Queue Information Example

This example shows how to get information about a particular queue in the Azure service bus
instance. The following screenshot shows the Anypoint Studio app flow for this example:

![App flow that shows the components used in the get Get Queue Information example](_images/azure-sb-mgmt-flow-get-queue.png)

Figure 1. App flow for the Get Queue Information example

1.  Create a new Mule project in Studio.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `http` and select the **Listener** operation:
3.  Drag the **Listener** operation onto the Studio canvas.
4.  In the **Listener** configuration, click **+** next to the **Connector configuration** field to
    add a global element.
5.  Accept the defaults.
6.  Set the **Path** field to `/queue`.

### Add and Configure the Get Entity Operation Example

Add the **Get Entity** operation to see information about the specified queue, including
configuration information and information about the metadata:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `azure` and select the Azure Service Bus Management
    Connector **Get Entity** operation.
2.  Drag the **Get Entity** operation onto the Studio canvas, to the right of **Listener**.
3.  In the **Get Entity** configuration, click **+** next to the **Connector configuration** field
    to add a global element.
4.  Enter values for the **SMS Namespace**, **Shared Access Key Name**, and **Shared Access Key**
    fields.
5.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the Azure Service Bus instance:
    - If the connection is successful, click **OK** to save the configuration.
    - Otherwise, review to correct any incorrect parameters, and test again.

6.  In the **Entity(Queue or Topic)** field, enter the name of the queue for which you want
    information.

### Add a Transform Message Component

Add a **Transform Message** component to format the data and show how the metadata is rendered for
the output of the **Get Entity** operation:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `transform`:
2.  Drag the **Transform Message** component onto the canvas, to the right of the **Get Entity**
    component.
3.  In the **Transform Message** configuration, change the display name to `Getting Data`.
4.  Overlay the brackets in the **Output** section with this XML:

    ```xml
    %dw 2.0
    output application/json
    ns ns0 http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom
    ns ns01 http://schemas.microsoft.com/netservices/2010/10/servicebus/connect
    ---
    {
    "title": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#title,
    "publishedAt": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#published,
    "lockDuration": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#QueueDescription.ns01#LockDuration,
    "maxSize": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#QueueDescription.ns01#MaxSizeInMegabytes,
    "requiresDuplicate": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#QueueDescription.ns01#RequiresDuplicateDetection,
    "requiresSession": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#QueueDescription.ns01#RequiresSession,
    "deadLettering": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#QueueDescription.ns01#DeadLetteringOnMessageExpiration,
    "enabledBatched": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#QueueDescription.ns01#EnableBatchedOperations,
    "defaultMTTL": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#QueueDescription.ns01#DefaultMessageTimeToLive,
    "duplicateDetection": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#QueueDescription.ns01#DuplicateDetectionHistoryTimeWindow,
    }
    ```

### Add a Logger Component

Add a Logger component to show the response in the **Mule Console**:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `logger` and select the **Logger (Core)** component.
2.  Drag the **Logger (Core)** component onto the Studio canvas, to the right of the **Transform
    Message** component.
3.  In the **Message** field of the Logger configuration, enter `#[payload]`.
4.  Save the project.
5.  Test the app by sending a GET request to `http://127.0.0.1:8081/queue`.

### XML Code for the Get Queue Example

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
xmlns:azure-service-bus-management="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-management"
xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-management http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-management/current/mule-azure-service-bus-management.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd">
<azure-service-bus-management:config name="Azure_Service_Bus_Management_Connector_Config" doc:name="Azure Service Bus Management Connector Config"  >
<azure-service-bus-management:shared-access-signature-connection namespace="azure-management-testing" sharedAccessKeyName="RootManageSharedAccessKey" sharedAccessKey="FgBRI++kphTGJcr2OL8G3BLLAdAn3p7newgQ2Ixf7hk="/>
</azure-service-bus-management:config>
<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" >
<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
</http:listener-config>
<flow name="azure-management-flow"  >
<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="queue"/>
<azure-service-bus-management:get-by-entity doc:name="Get Entity" config-ref="Azure_Service_Bus_Management_Connector_Config" entity="your-queue"/>
<ee:transform doc:name="Getting data"  >
<ee:message >
<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
ns ns0 http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom
ns ns01 http://schemas.microsoft.com/netservices/2010/10/servicebus/connect
---
{
"title": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#title,
"publishedAt": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#published,
"lockDuration": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#QueueDescription.ns01#LockDuration,
"maxSize": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#QueueDescription.ns01#MaxSizeInMegabytes,
"requiresDuplicate": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#QueueDescription.ns01#RequiresDuplicateDetection,
"requiresSession": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#QueueDescription.ns01#RequiresSession,
"deadLettering": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#QueueDescription.ns01#DeadLetteringOnMessageExpiration,
"enabledBatched": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#QueueDescription.ns01#EnableBatchedOperations,
"defaultMTTL": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#QueueDescription.ns01#DefaultMessageTimeToLive,
"duplicateDetection": payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#QueueDescription.ns01#DuplicateDetectionHistoryTimeWindow,
}]]></ee:set-payload>
</ee:message>
</ee:transform>
<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger"  message="#[payload]"/>
</flow>
</mule>
```

## Create Topic Example

This example shows how to create a new topic for the Azure Service Bus instance. The following
screenshot shows the Anypoint Studio flow for this example:

![App flow that shows the components used in the Create Topic example](_images/azure-sb-mgmt-flow-create-topic.png)

Figure 2. App flow for the Create Topic example

1.  Create a new Mule project in Studio.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `http` and select the **Listener** operation:
3.  Drag the **Listener** operation onto the canvas.
4.  In the **Listener** configuration, click **+** next to the **Connector configuration** field to
    add a global element.
5.  Set the **Host** field to 0.0.0.0, the **Port** field to `808`, and click **Save**.
6.  Set the **Path** field to `/topic`.

### Add and Configure the Create or Update Entity Operation

The **Create or Update Entity** operation enables you to create the topic:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `azure service bus management` and select the **Create
    or Update Entity** operation.
2.  Drag the **Create or Update Entity** operation onto the canvas, to the right of **Listener**.
3.  In the **Create or Update Entity** configuration, click **+** next to the **Connector
    configuration** field to add a global element.
4.  Enter values for the **SMS Namespace**, **Shared Access Key Name**, and **Shared Access Key**
    fields.
5.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the Azure Service Bus instance:
    - If the connection is successful, click **OK** to save the configuration.
    - Otherwise, review or correct any incorrect parameters, and test again.

6.  In the **Entity(Queue or Topic)** field, enter the name of the queue for which you want
    information.

### Add a Transform Message Component

Add a **Transform Message** component to format the data and show how the metadata is rendered for
the output of the **Create or Update Entity** operation:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `transform`:
2.  Drag the **Transform Message** component onto the canvas, to the right of the **Create or Update
    Entity** component.
3.  In the **Transform Message** configuration, change the display name to `Getting Data`.
4.  Overlay the brackets in the **Output** section with this XML:

    ```xml
    %dw 2.0
    output application/json
    ns ns0 http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom
    ns ns01 http://schemas.microsoft.com/netservices/2010/10/servicebus/connect
    ---
    {
    title: payload.ns0#entry.ns0#title,
    publishedAt: payload.ns0#entry.ns0#published,
    maxSize: payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#TopicDescription.ns01#MaxSizeInMegabytes,
    requiresDuplicate: payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#TopicDescription.ns01#RequiresDuplicateDetection,
    enableBatched: payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#TopicDescription.ns01#EnableBatchedOperations,
    defaultMTTL: payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#TopicDescription.ns01#DefaultMessageTimeToLive,
    duplicateDetection: payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#TopicDescription.ns01#DuplicateDetectionHistoryTimeWindow,
    }
    ```

### Add a Logger Component

The Logger component displays the new topic in the **Mule Console**:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `logger` and select the **Logger (Core)** component.
2.  Drag the **Logger (Core)** component onto the Studio canvas, to the right of the **Transform
    Message** component.
3.  In the **Message** field of the Logger configuration, enter `#[payload]`.
4.  Save the project.
5.  Test the app by sending a GET request to `http://127.0.0.1:8081/topic`.

### XML for the Create Topic Example

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
xmlns:azure-service-bus-management="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-management"
xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-management http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-management/current/mule-azure-service-bus-management.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd">
<azure-service-bus-management:config name="Azure_Service_Bus_Management_Connector_Config" doc:name="Azure Service Bus Management Connector Config" doc:id="a59bdeee-75d3-4708-b3e1-923189366fcf" >
<azure-service-bus-management:shared-access-signature-connection namespace="azure-management-
ing" sharedAccessKeyName="RootManageSharedAccessKey" sharedAccessKey="FgBRI++kphTGJcr2OL8G3BLLAdAn3p7newgQ2Ixf7hk="/>
</azure-service-bus-management:config>
<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="d3ad3e78-e927-47fd-9aa9-997c5d3cb5ff" >
<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
</http:listener-config>
<flow name="azure-management-flow" doc:id="2ca28772-b9ce-44ec-a0d3-68568789cb8e" >
<http:listener doc:name="Listener" doc:id="60a97736-63e4-42bb-8487-e656cad985d6" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="topic"/>
<azure-service-bus-management:update-by-entity doc:name="Create or Update Entity" doc:id="b7bda909-831f-4e05-b4da-810adb8b541d" config-ref="Azure_Service_Bus_Management_Connector_Config" entity="topicName">
<azure-service-bus-management:content ><![CDATA[#['<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<entry xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom">
<content type="application/xml">
<TopicDescription xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/netservices/2010/10/servicebus/connect">
<MaxSizeInMegabytes>1024</MaxSizeInMegabytes>
<RequiresDuplicateDetection>false</RequiresDuplicateDetection>
<EnableBatchedOperations>false</EnableBatchedOperations>
<DefaultMessageTimeToLive>PT256204778H48M5S</DefaultMessageTimeToLive>
<DuplicateDetectionHistoryTimeWindow>PT10M</DuplicateDetectionHistoryTimeWindow>
</TopicDescription>
</content>
</entry>']]]></azure-service-bus-management:content>
</azure-service-bus-management:update-by-entity>
<ee:transform doc:name="Getting data" doc:id="67b79a1a-2eec-4ed5-a160-c44b14da829b" >
<ee:message >
<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
ns ns0 http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom
ns ns01 http://schemas.microsoft.com/netservices/2010/10/servicebus/connect
---
{
title: payload.ns0#entry.ns0#title,
publishedAt: payload.ns0#entry.ns0#published,
maxSize: payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#TopicDescription.ns01#MaxSizeInMegabytes,
requiresDuplicate: payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#TopicDescription.ns01#RequiresDuplicateDetection,
enableBatched: payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#TopicDescription.ns01#EnableBatchedOperations,
defaultMTTL: payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#TopicDescription.ns01#DefaultMessageTimeToLive,
duplicateDetection: payload.ns0#entry.ns0#content.ns01#TopicDescription.ns01#DuplicateDetectionHistoryTimeWindow,
}]]></ee:set-payload>
</ee:message>
</ee:transform>
<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" doc:id="577d2989-1b0b-4c95-a16b-3cce4b4ccaca" message="#[payload]"/>
</flow>
</mule>
```

# Azure Service Bus Management Connector 1.0 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Support Version: Select

## Configurations

---

### Config

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Config_SharedAccessSignature">Shared Access Signature</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit for the Response Timeout</p></td><td><p>MINUTES</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether to send the request using streaming. Set this value to <code>AUTO</code> to have the connector automatically use the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td><p>AUTO</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The identifier used to reference this element in other components</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the platform expires the instance at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Shared Access Signature

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultQueryParam">Default Query Param</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for establishing connections to the remote service</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Connection Timeout</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If false, each connection is closed after the first request completes.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of outbound connections to keep open at the same time</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A timeout for how long a connection can remain idle before it is closed</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>A reusable configuration element for outbound connections through a proxy</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether or not received responses should be streamed</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The space, in bytes, for the buffer where the HTTP response is stored.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Service Namespace</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The Azure Service Bus namespace</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Shared Access Key Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the access key configured on the namespace</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Shared Access Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The 256-bit primary key</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Protocol to use for communication. Valid values are HTTP and HTTPS</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">Tls</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Operations

- [Delete Entity](#DeleteByEntity)
- [Delete Subscription](#DeleteSubscriptionsByTopicSubscription)
- [Delete Rule](#DeleteSubscriptionsRulesByTopicSubscriptionRule)
- [Get Entity](#GetByEntity)
- [List Queues](#GetResourcesQueues)
- [List Topics](#GetResourcesTopics)
- [List Subscriptions](#GetSubscriptionsByTopic)
- [Get Subscription](#GetSubscriptionsByTopicSubscription)
- [List Rules](#GetSubscriptionsRulesByTopicSubscription)
- [Get Rule](#GetSubscriptionsRulesByTopicSubscriptionRule)
- [Create or Update Entity](#UpdateByEntity)
- [Create or update Subscription](#UpdateSubscriptionsByTopicSubscription)
- [Create or Update Rule](#UpdateSubscriptionsRulesByTopicSubscriptionRule)

## Delete Entity

`<azure-service-bus-management:delete-by-entity>`

Deletes the specified entity in the service bus instance. This operation makes an `HTTP DELETE`
request to the `/{entity}` endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entity (Queue or Topic)</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The queue or topic to delete</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit for the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether to send the request using streaming. Set this value to <code>AUTO</code> to have the connector automatically use the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy if there are connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:UNAUTHORIZED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Delete Subscription

`<azure-service-bus-management:delete-subscriptions-by-topic-subscription>`

Deletes a subscription over a specified topic. This operation makes an `HTTP DELETE` request to the
`/{topic}/Subscriptions/{subscription}` endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Topic</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Topic for which to delete a subscription</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Topic subscription for which to delete a subscription</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit for the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether to send the request using streaming. Set this value to <code>AUTO</code> to have the connector automatically use the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy if there are connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:UNAUTHORIZED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Delete Rule

`<azure-service-bus-management:delete-subscriptions-rules-by-topic-subscription-rule>`

Delete a rule from the service bus instance over a specified subscription and topic. This operation
makes an `HTTP DELETE` request to the `/{topic}/Subscriptions/{subscription}/rules/{rule}` endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Topic</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Topic</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The topic subscription</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Rule</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The subscription rule</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit for the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether to send the request using streaming. Set this value to <code>AUTO</code> to have the connector automatically use the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy if there are connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:UNAUTHORIZED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get Entity

`<azure-service-bus-management:get-by-entity>`

Retrieves the specified queue or topic from the service bus instance. This operation makes an
`HTTP GET` request to the `/{entity}` endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entity (Queue or Topic)</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The queue or topic to retrieve.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Disables the repeatable stream functionality and uses non-repeatable streams, which can have less performance overhead, memory use, and cost</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit for the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether the request should be sent using streaming. Set this value to <code>AUTO</code> to have the connector automatically use the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy if there are connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:UNAUTHORIZED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## List Queues

`<azure-service-bus-management:get-resources-queues>`

Retrieves all existing queues from the service bus instance. This operation makes an `HTTP GET`
request to the `/$Resources/Queues` endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Disables the repeatable stream functionality and uses non-repeatable streams, which can have less performance overhead, memory use, and cost</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit for the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether the request should be sent using streaming. Set this value to <code>AUTO</code> to have the connector automatically use the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy if there are connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:UNAUTHORIZED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## List Topics

`<azure-service-bus-management:get-resources-topics>`

Retrieves all existing topics from the service bus instance. This operation makes an `HTTP GET`
request to the `/$Resources/Topics` endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Disables the repeatable stream functionality and uses non-repeatable streams, which can have less performance overhead, memory use, and cost</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit for the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether to send the request using streaming. Set this value to <code>AUTO</code> to have the connector automatically use the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy if there are connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:UNAUTHORIZED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## List Subscriptions

`<azure-service-bus-management:get-subscriptions-by-topic>`

Retrieves all existing subscriptions of the specific topic from the service bus instance. This
operation makes an `HTTP GET` request to the `/{topic}/Subscriptions` endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Topic</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The Topic to retrieve all subscriptions</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Disables the repeatable stream functionality and uses non-repeatable streams, which can have less performance overhead, memory use, and cost</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit for the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether to send the request using streaming. Set this value to <code>AUTO</code> to have the connector automatically use the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy if there are connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:UNAUTHORIZED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get Subscription

`<azure-service-bus-management:get-subscriptions-by-topic-subscription>`

Retrieves the specified subscription. This operation makes an `HTTP GET` request to the
`/{topic}/Subscriptions/{subscription}` endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Topic</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The topic of the subscription</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The subscription to retrieve</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Disables the repeatable stream functionality and uses non-repeatable streams, which can have less performance overhead, memory use, and cost</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit for the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether to send the request using streaming. Set this value to <code>AUTO</code> to have the connector automatically use the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy if there are connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MANAGEMENT:TIMEOUT
